key_insight: A physics-informed, boundary-masked attention mechanism improves out-of-distribution grain-growth prediction by focusing the model on grain-boundary pixels, yielding better robustness than a standard deep learning baseline.
engineering_relevance: This suggests grain-growth models trained on synthetic data can be made more reliable for real microstructure evolution prediction, especially when used in materials design and process optimization workflows.
method: Evaluated a pre-trained deep learning grain-growth model on three OOD cases: experimental microstructures, bimodal grain size distributions, and abnormal grain growth.
Added a boundary-masked attention mechanism that restricts attention to grain-boundary pixels.
Compared baseline vs. physics-informed attention without retraining or fine-tuning on OOD data.
evidence_signal: Both models generalized to all three test cases, but the boundary-masked attention model gave the largest gain on bimodal microstructures, improving SSIM from 0.6221 to 0.7609 and reducing mean grain-size error from 8.75% to 3.57%.
watch_item: The added benefit depends on how well the target morphology matches the training domain, so performance may be less reliable for strongly novel microstructures.
paper_title: Physics-Informed Attention Mechanism and Generalization Capability of Deep Learning-Based Grain Growth Evolution Prediction
